demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse, who is present for this call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My skin feels like it's turning into a tight suit of armor, and since yesterday I can't walk two steps without gasping for air and coughing dryly.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The breathlessness got much worse over the last day or two — he's short of breath just sitting in the chair now, and the skin tightness is really painful. His spouse called because they're frightened about his breathing tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: the skin tightening has crept in over about 3 months; the breathlessness took a sharp turn for the worse in the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: months of skin changes and a long-standing dry cough, with the breathing much worse over 1-2 days
symptom_narrative.character: skin feels thick, tight and darker on the chest and arms, like it's shrinking; a dry, hacking cough with no phlegm; breathing feels like he can't get enough air
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: he says it's the worst the breathing has ever been — he can't walk more than a couple of steps without stopping to gasp
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: tight skin over the chest and both arms; breathlessness and cough in the chest; dry gritty eyes and a dry mouth
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant now and worse with any movement; worse over the last two days
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting still and resting helps the breathing a little; a humidifier and moisturiser help the skin and dryness a bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any exertion at all makes the breathlessness much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: very dry, gritty eyes and a very dry mouth with almost no saliva; stiff, hard-to-bend elbows from the tight skin
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the cough and skin changes have been building for months but he has never been this breathless before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can now only get from the bed to the chair; the spouse is helping with all moving about

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: had a donor stem cell (bone marrow) transplant about 14 months ago for leukaemia
past_medical_history.how_managed: followed up by the transplant team; on immune-suppressing tablets since

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: skin has been getting thick and tight, and eyes and mouth very dry, since a few months after the transplant
past_medical_history.how_managed: moisturisers, eye drops and a humidifier at home

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a dry cough that has been there for a while and has now got much worse

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: an immune-suppressing tablet (he thinks it is called tacrolimus)
thick moisturising ointment for the skin

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse who is the informant and carer tonight; high anxiety about his breathing and his transplant

communication_style.typical_message_style: short, breathless answers; spouse fills in the detail

objective_data_offered.value: widespread thick, tight, darker skin across the chest and both arms; stiff elbows that do not fully straighten; restricted, mask-like facial movement
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: appearance over a video call, not a hands-on skin examination

objective_data_offered.value: visibly short of breath and gasping after only a small movement; frequent dry cough; breathing looks fast
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: visual impression only; true respiratory rate and oxygen level not measurable on video

objective_data_offered.value: spouse reports he cannot walk more than a couple of steps without stopping to catch his breath, and is breathless even sitting still tonight
eyes feel very dry and gritty and the mouth is very dry with almost no saliva
a humidifier is running in the bedroom
